title: Oberna littoralis
intro: Oberna littoralis, also known as the Coastal Oberna, is a plant species belonging to the family Plumbaginaceae. This plant is valued for its traditional medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Oberna littoralis include plumbagin, a naphthoquinone with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. In traditional medicine, Oberna littoralis is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Oberna littoralis is a member of the family Scrophulariaceae, but it is now often placed in the family Plantaginaceae. It belongs to the genus Oberna. The species is classified as a flowering plant, with Oberna littoralis being a type of annual or perennial herb. Further classification places Oberna littoralis in the order Lamiales. It is a part of the family of plants known as dicotyledons.
